game_name: Master of Orion II: Battle at Antares

danger_signals.visual_form: Increasingly powerful enemy fleets materializing from dimensional portal
danger_signals.meaning: Existential threat to player colonies; late-game catastrophic danger phase

danger_signals.visual_form: Extremely powerful robotic warship defending Orion homeworld
danger_signals.meaning: High-difficulty military objective; protected strategic location

danger_signals.visual_form: Hostile creatures occupying desirable star systems
danger_signals.meaning: Early-game hazard; barrier to resource-rich territory acquisition

danger_signals.visual_form: Food deficit indicator in colony management interface
danger_signals.meaning: Population loss imminent; colony failure without intervention

safety_signals.visual_form: System ownership color coding on galaxy map
safety_signals.meaning: Player or ally territory; safe for resource extraction and expansion

safety_signals.visual_form: Positive food economy indicator in colony readout
safety_signals.meaning: Population growth enabled; colony stability and expansion capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Defended colony with stationed fleet presence
safety_signals.meaning: Military protection against invasion; reduced vulnerability

faction_indicators.color_identity: User-selectable empire color on galaxy map
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Hostile ship designs differentiate by player race
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Colonized planets display empire color; infrastructure buildings race-specific

faction_indicators.color_identity: Each race has distinct color palette for empire identification
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Race portraits and ship designs provide visual faction markers
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Ships, structures, and UI elements visually differentiate hostile vs. allied races
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Conquered/colonized worlds show conquering race's visual theme

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Orion star system as legendary location; Guardian warship as iconic artifact
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Orion homeworld contains Avenger starship and unique Antaran technologies
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Orion homeworld is central strategic location with visual dominance

faction_indicators.color_identity: Dimensional/otherworldly visual aesthetic distinct from normal space
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Dimensional Portal as visual gateway; pocket dimension realm marker
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Antaran fleets progressively increase in visual complexity/threat representation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Portal location marks Antaran incursion point; realm appearance distinct from known space

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fog of war obscures unexplored star systems; limited visible territory on galaxy map
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Space monsters present in high-value systems; weak player fleets vulnerable to creature encounters

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple colonized systems visible in player color; rival empires occupying adjacent systems with distinct colors
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Diplomatic interactions visible through alliance/hostility state; fleet movements between systems

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dimensional Portal becomes visible on map; Antaran fleet markers appear with escalating visual intensity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increasingly powerful enemy fleets attack player colonies; portal generates repeated invasion waves

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All visible star systems display player empire color; rival empire colors absent from map
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No enemy fleets visible; all remaining forces are player-controlled

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Pop-up UI shows galactic vote tally; player achieves 2/3 majority visual confirmation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Rival AI empires cease hostile actions; peaceful coexistence indicated

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dimensional Portal conquered; Antaran homeworld captured shows player color control
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Antaran fleet spawning ceases; portal becomes controlled territory

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Colony readout displays red/negative food indicator; population counter decreases
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Colonist starvation progresses visually; population shrinks until intervention

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Government type displayed in empire profile; bonuses/penalties shown in tech/economy readouts
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Research speed, wealth generation, espionage vulnerability vary by government; visual performance metrics reflect state

environmental_storytelling.example: Orion homeworld contains Avenger starship and non-researchable Antaran technologies; visual discovery of alien relics tells story of Orion-Antaran war
Orion star system is heavily guarded by Guardian warship; environmental hazard design indicates location's historical significance
Portal appearance marks transition from exploration phase to existential threat; visual appearance of dimensional rift communicates otherworldly danger
Different planet types (gas giants requiring construction tech, asteroids, solid worlds) indicate galactic diversity and resource distribution strategy
Shared systems with opponents create territorial visual conflict; multiple colonizable planets in single system tell story of resource competition
Desirable star systems occupied by creatures indicate ecological barriers and natural selection of optimal worlds

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Colonists reallocate between farming, industry, and research; visual output shifts in colony readouts
Spaceship designs visible in construction queue; fleet movements between systems visible on galaxy map
No hostile fleet movements; diplomatic relation status shown in UI; trade agreements visible
Fleet movements toward player territory; attack formations visible in tactical combat interface
Initial fleet wave emerges from dimensional portal; moderate power level visible in fleet composition
Progressively larger and more powerful fleets spawn from portal; escalating visual complexity of Antaran vessel designs
Population growth indicated in readout; positive food surplus; expanding visual city/infrastructure
Food deficit shows in red; population counter static or declining; visual infrastructure appears sparse/underdeveloped
Stationary occupation of high-value system; attacks player fleets that approach; visual presence blocks resource access

notes: Master of Orion II communicates world-state primarily through color-coded galaxy map (empire territories), UI readouts (colony economy, fleet composition), and progressive threat visualization (Antaran escalation). Victory conditions are visually distinct: military victory shows total map color dominance; diplomatic victory triggers election UI confirmation; Antaran victory requires portal capture. Government type affects visual performance metrics (research speed bars, wealth generation graphs). Multi-planet systems and shared territories create visual territorial tension. Three victory routes create distinct late-game visual states.
